chunk_summary: This section outlines a stepwise algorithm for managing feeding challenges in neurodisability, integrating triage, action, and follow-up, with conservative failure criteria and escalation strategies for nutrition and hydration support.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise approach to managing feeding difficulties in neurodisability using an integrated algorithm.
Identify conservative failure criteria for feeding plans and their clinical implications.
Learn when to escalate to non-oral hydration or nutrition, including short-term NG/NJ trials and gastrostomy planning.
Recognize the importance of individualized plans and follow-up for tracking outcomes like growth, hydration, and caregiver burden.
key_facts: Baseline function should be classified using GMFCS and EDACS (or MINI-EDACS for ages 18-36 months).
Conservative failure criteria include ongoing cough, choking, weight decline, dehydration, or prolonged mealtimes overwhelming caregivers, reviewed within 2-6 weeks.
Inpatient evaluation is necessary for suspected pneumonia requiring oxygen, unsafe hydration, or caregiver inability to execute plans.
Texture modification should follow posture, bolus pacing, utensil changes, and environmental adjustments, per NICE guidance.
Non-oral nutrition escalation should begin with short-term NG/NJ trials before considering long-term gastrostomy.
reference_quote: NICE guidance emphasizes individualized plans for texture modification in children with cerebral palsy (Reference 1).
document_key_topics: Conservative failure criteria for feeding plans
Escalation to non-oral nutrition and hydration
